idea.reasoning: Pinned idea for direct validation – Loadify.ai mobile QA automation
idea.description: Loadify – AI-powered mobile QA automation for outsourcing agencies. AI agents run test scenarios on real iOS/Android devices, auto-generate test cases from requirements, execute with pass/fail flags, and keep tests up-to-date when requirements change. Targets outsourcing companies (5-50 people) in Russia/CIS who do mobile app development for clients. Pricing: $1000/month SaaS or $2000-5000/month done-for-you testing service. Working alpha already exists. Value prop: fewer QA headcount, more bugs caught, scale client load without hiring.

landing_page.headline: Cut QA Costs & Scale Without Hiring More Testers
landing_page.subheadline: For Russia/CIS mobile app outsourcing agencies: automate testing, catch more bugs, and handle more clients without expanding QA team
features: AI auto-generates test cases from client requirements in minutes
Runs tests on real iOS/Android devices with pass/fail flags
Auto-updates test scenarios when requirements change
Catches 2-3x more bugs before release vs manual testing
Scales your testing capacity without hiring additional QA staff

persona.name: Dmitri Volkov
persona.current_workflow: Manual test case creation in Excel, execution on physical devices shared across team, bug tracking in Jira, regression testing takes 2-3 weeks per release, constantly firefighting client complaints about missed bugs
persona.current_tools: Jira, TestRail, BrowserStack, Slack, GitLab, Firebase Crashlytics, Sentry

top_struggles: QA bottleneck slowing client deliveries
Client retention threatened by bugs in production
Cannot scale testing without hiring more QA staff
daily_life.daily_routine: Arrive 8:30, standup 9:00, code review and architecture meetings until noon, afternoon for client calls and resource planning, evening checking CI/CD pipelines and test results
daily_life.discovery_context: Searching for 'mobile app automation testing tools' and 'AI testing tools' during late night research after another client complaint about missed bugs
current_priorities: Reduce QA cycle time by 50%
Win more client contracts without overloading team
Prove ROI to keep current clients happy

friction_points: $5000/month is MORE expensive than hiring a QA engineer ($2000-4000)
Would blow through my entire software budget plus $4500 more
No time to properly evaluate - Im overwhelmed with 14hr workdays
Need to see proven results from similar CIS agencies first
Would need to consult with business partner before this spend
AI test generation claims sound too good - need case studies
simulations.reasoning: The tool directly addresses my QA bottleneck and client retention problems, which keeps me up at night. However at $5K/month its more expensive than just hiring a real QA person, and it blows way past my software budget. With only 1.5 hours free daily and 8.5 months runway I cant justify this risk without seeing proof other CIS agencies succeeded. Id likely trial it if they offered a free pilot, but I wont make an impulse decision on a $5K commitment right now.
simulations.competing_with: Hiring a junior QA engineer for $2500/month

persona.name: Ekaterina "Katya" Petrova
persona.current_workflow: Manual testing with scattered Appium scripts that are rarely updated. Test cases written in TestRail, executed manually by 4 QA testers. Bug reports written in Jira with screenshots. When client requirements change, test cases become outdated within days and nobody has time to update them. Regression testing takes 2+ weeks per release.
persona.current_tools: Jira, TestRail, Firebase Crashlytics, Appium (underutilized), Slack, Google Sheets, Zoom

top_struggles: Manual regression testing consumes 40% of sprint time
Test cases become stale within days of client changes
Can't scale QA team fast enough to meet client demand
daily_life.daily_routine: 07:30 wake, 08:30 commute to office, 09:00-10:00 daily standup with dev + QA team, 10:00-12:00 review bug reports and assign to testers, 12:00-13:00 lunch, 13:00-16:00 client calls and project status updates, 16:00-18:00 coordinate testing across 3 active client projects, 18:00-19:00 update stakeholders, 19:00 leave office, 20:00-23:30 answer Slack messages and plan next day
daily_life.discovery_context: Researching during late-night hours after putting kids to bed. Found Loadify through a LinkedIn post from a fellow PM in Kyiv. Also received cold email but initially deleted it. Revisited after seeing discussion in QA leaders Telegram group.
current_priorities: Reduce manual regression testing time by 50%
Win 2 new client contracts next quarter
Retain existing QA talent (2 testers gave notice recently)

friction_points: Recently made a bad purchase decision - skeptical of new tools
No time to properly evaluate and implement - overwhelmed with current workload
Need to see actual proof it works on our specific client projects before committing
Worried about learning curve taking away from sprint delivery
Would need buy-in from partner/co-founder before spending $2000/month
simulations.reasoning: The tool directly addresses my biggest pain points (manual regression eating 40% of sprint, stale test cases), and $2000 is cheaper than hiring a QA engineer. But after my recent bad purchase, I can't justify another $2k/month without running the 14-day trial first and seeing actual results. I'm also too overwhelmed right now to dedicate time to learning a new system - I'd need to block off several hours I simply don't have until next sprint starts.

persona.name: Alexei Volkov
persona.current_workflow: We rely heavily on manual testing with 2 junior testers who rotate between projects. When requirements change, we have to retest everything from scratch. Clients complain about bugs slipping through, and I'm constantly firefighting instead of growing the business. We sometimes use freelance QA via Upwork for big releases but it's inconsistent.
persona.current_tools: Jira for project tracking (we use free tier), Google Sheets for test cases, manual device testing on our team phones, occasionally BrowserStack when clients insist but it's too expensive to use regularly

top_struggles: QA delays causing missed deadlines and client dissatisfaction
Can't afford dedicated QA team but clients demand quality
Requirements change constantly and we waste time re-testing
daily_life.daily_routine: Starts with client emails and standups, spends most of day managing developers and handling QA fires, ends with administrative tasks and proposal writing. Works Saturdays to keep up.
daily_life.discovery_context: Searched for 'affordable mobile app automation testing' after a client threatened to leave due to bugs. Also asks in founder groups for tool recommendations.
current_priorities: Retaining current clients and improving quality
Hiring one more developer to take on more work
Finding ways to reduce time spent on QA without hiring

friction_points: $2000/month is 75% of my entire profit margin - too risky after my recent bad purchase
I have zero time to implement and learn new tools with my current schedule
Need to see proven results from similar CIS agencies first before risking this much
My cash runway is only 4.2 months - can't afford another mistake
simulations.reasoning: The problem this solves is exactly my biggest pain point - QA delays are killing my client relationships. However, $2000/month is nearly my entire profit and I just recovered from a bad purchase decision last month. I need to see this work for someone like me first. I'd try the trial but realistically I won't have time to implement it properly until next month, and by then I'll probably talk myself out of it due to the cost.
simulations.competing_with: manual testing (current habit) and skepticism from recent bad purchase

When requirements change, manually updating test documentation takes 2-3 days.
persona.current_tools: Jira (project tracking), GitHub (code), Firebase (distribution), BrowserStack (limited cross-browser testing), Notion (test docs), Figma (design specs)

top_struggles: Developers wasting time on manual testing instead of coding
Client complaints about bugs slipping through to production

daily_life.discovery_context: Reading tech blogs and LinkedIn posts about AI development tools, also hears about tools through fellow founders in local startup meetups in Kazan
current_priorities: Delivering current client project on time

What happens to existing test cases if we switch? Migration complexity unclear

I'd need to see integration docs and at least one case study from a similar CIS agency before even starting a trial. Probably would push this to next month after we finish the current client交付.

simulations.reasoning: The problem this solves is real - we're losing developer time to manual testing and clients are complaining about bugs. But $5000/month is a huge stretch when our net is only $8000 and I barely have 1.5 hours free. The claims sound promising but I need to see actual results from similar CIS agencies, not just marketing copy. Would need my technical lead to vet the AI claims and probably a longer trial to validate. Right now I can't afford the mental bandwidth to properly evaluate this tool.
simulations.competing_with: manual QA processes and spreadsheet tracking

pinned_ideas.reasoning: Pinned idea for direct validation – Loadify.ai mobile QA automation
pinned_ideas.description: Loadify – AI-powered mobile QA automation for outsourcing agencies. AI agents run test scenarios on real iOS/Android devices, auto-generate test cases from requirements, execute with pass/fail flags, and keep tests up-to-date when requirements change. Targets outsourcing companies (5-50 people) in Russia/CIS who do mobile app development for clients. Pricing: $1000/month SaaS or $2000-5000/month done-for-you testing service. Working alpha already exists. Value prop: fewer QA headcount, more bugs caught, scale client load without hiring.
